evidence: `src/integrations/supabase/client.ts` performs runtime work at import time via `export const supabase = getOrCreateSupabaseClient()`, and that path installs instrumentation and initializes runtime.
The same module mutates global window state (`window.__supabase_client__`, `window.supabase`), coupling all importers to browser-global singleton behavior.

`src/shared/lib/errorHandling/handleError.ts` (shared lib) imports UI toast (`@/shared/components/ui/toast`), forcing non-UI callers to depend on presentation concerns through a high-fan-in error utility.

`src/types/shots.ts` is a deprecated compatibility barrel, while assigned consumers like `src/shared/components/MediaGallery/hooks/useMediaGalleryHandlers.ts` still import through the old boundary (`@/types/shots`).
`src/pages/Home/components/InstallInstructionsModal.tsx` is a 441-line component that combines modal orchestration with many platform-specific browser mockup subcomponents in one file.
`src/shared/hooks/useMobile.tsx` combines UA parsing utilities, multiple narrow hooks, and a composite device-state hook in one shared module, making ownership of device policy diffuse.
The deprecated type entrypoint (`src/types/shots.ts`) remains part of active flow via `useMediaGalleryHandlers`, indicating top-level ownership boundaries are still transitional rather than crisp.
Task row mapping is duplicated with near-identical logic in `src/shared/hooks/useTasks.ts` (`mapDbTaskToTask`) and `src/shared/lib/generationTaskBridge.ts` (`mapDbTaskRowToTask`).

Timestamp hooks (`useCompletedTimestamp`, `useProcessingTimestamp`, `useUpdatingTimestamp`) are thin wrappers over `useLiveRelativeTimestamp` with mostly formatter/name differences, increasing surface area for small behavior deltas.
Task creation modules (`imageGeneration`, `magicEdit`, `zImageTurboI2I`, `joinClips`) each re-implement similar validate/build/create scaffolding instead of sharing stronger typed builder primitives.

Task-generation orchestration is split across multiple seam layers (`generationTaskBridge`, `generationTaskCache`, `generationTaskRepository`) with overlapping responsibilities and divergent status propagation, producing integration drift rather than a single translation boundary (src/shared/lib/generationTaskBridge.ts, src/shared/lib/generationTaskCache.ts, src/shared/lib/generationTaskRepository.ts).

Travel-generation payload assembly is split between `segmentSettingsUtils.buildTaskParams` and `generateVideoService.buildTravelRequestBody`, each encoding similar domain concepts with separate mapping logic and defaults, increasing glue-code entropy at boundaries (src/shared/components/segmentSettingsUtils.ts:96-157, src/tools/travel-between-images/components/ShotEditor/services/generateVideoService.ts:463-589).

Invalidation seam has three different execution models (hook with cancelable timer map, sync helper with fire-and-forget timer, async variant flow with awaited refetch), making lifecycle behavior less predictable across callers (src/shared/hooks/invalidation/useGenerationInvalidation.ts:24-141).

Critical multi-mode storage handoff paths (upload vs existing path vs thumbnail generation) are only smoke-tested for exports, leaving boundary behavior weakly protected against regressions (supabase/functions/complete_task/storage.ts:34-208, supabase/functions/complete_task/storage.test.ts:1-10).
`src/features/gallery/hooks/__tests__/useGalleryPageState.test.ts` and `src/shared/hooks/__tests__/useGalleryPageState.test.ts` are byte-identical duplicates (same 226-line suite).

`src/shared/hooks/__tests__/useAutoSaveSettings.test.ts` and `src/shared/settings/hooks/__tests__/useAutoSaveSettings.test.ts` contain largely duplicated test bodies with path-level divergence only, increasing maintenance drift risk.

Task modules (`magicEdit`, `zImageTurboI2I`, `imageGeneration`) repeat similar low-level validation and payload assembly patterns (URL checks, seed bounds, batch fan-out loops), which increases branch-level duplication.

Two parallel generation-task APIs expose the same function names (`preloadGenerationTaskMappings`, `enhanceGenerationsWithTaskData`) but with different cache contracts: `generationTaskBridge` writes `{ taskId }` while `generationTaskCache` writes `{ taskId, status }` (src/shared/lib/generationTaskBridge.ts:138-221, src/shared/lib/generationTaskCache.ts:28-118).

`buildTravelRequestBody` is intentionally overloaded with both nested parameter groups and legacy flat fields in one boundary function, increasing call-shape ambiguity (src/tools/travel-between-images/components/ShotEditor/services/generateVideoService.ts:463-589).

`getStoragePublicUrl` returns an `exists` boolean while explicitly documenting that existence is not verified, creating a misleading API contract (supabase/functions/complete_task/storage.ts:177-190).

Edge helper responses use mixed API shapes in one shared module: `parseJsonBody` returns an `OperationResult`, `parseJsonBodyStrict` returns `{ body|errorResponse }`, and `ensureRequestMethod` returns plain text 405 response (supabase/functions/_shared/edgeRequest.ts:49-196).
Several files use heavy explanatory/comment scaffolding around trivial helpers (e.g., src/shared/components/ui/cn.ts, src/shared/hooks/useUpdatingTimestamp.ts, src/shared/lib/caseConversion.ts).

Thin wrapper patterns appear with minimal added behavior, such as `cn(...)` forwarding to twMerge/clsx and `useTaskTimestamp(...)` forwarding to `useUpdatingTimestamp(...)` with a single formatter override.
Large doc blocks and migration narration in type-only modules (src/shared/components/ImageGenerationForm/types.ts, src/shared/lib/taskConfig.ts) add maintenance volume without enforcing behavior.

src/types/shots.ts is explicitly deprecated with a removal date (2026-05-31) but remains actively consumed (e.g., src/shared/components/ShotImageManager/utils/image-utils.ts imports `GenerationRow` from `@/types/shots`).

src/shared/lib/tasks/travelBetweenImages/legacyStructureVideo.ts defines a compatibility shim with many deprecated fields and a sunset contract, indicating old/new payload schemas are still coexisting.

src/shared/components/ImageGenerationForm/types.ts keeps multiple deprecated persisted settings fields (`selectedReferenceId`, `styleReferenceImage`, `subjectStrength`, etc.) for backward compatibility.

All 5 assigned root config files are marked peripheral with low fan-in in holistic_context.structure.root_files: vitest.edge.config.ts (fan_in 1), vitest.edge.smoke.config.ts (1), tailwind.config.ts (0), vite.config.ts (0), vitest.config.ts (0).

Holistic structure already has dedicated config directories (`config/testing/`, `config/vite/`, `config/tailwind/theme/`), but equivalent concern files remain at repository root, creating split placement for the same responsibility.
vitest.config.ts imports `./vitest.edge.config`, showing config-to-config dependency while both remain as root stragglers instead of a single testing-config package.

Lightbox editing flow is split across very large modules with broad pass-through state contracts: `src/shared/components/MediaLightbox/ImageLightbox.tsx` (~778 lines) and `src/shared/components/MediaLightbox/hooks/useImageEditOrchestrator.ts` (~541 lines).

`useImageEditOrchestrator` composes multiple mode systems (inpaint, magic edit, reposition, img2img), then re-exports a very wide context object and dependency list (notably lines 392-524), indicating orchestration + state-shaping + UI contract responsibilities in one hook.

`src/tools/travel-between-images/components/Timeline.tsx` (~587 lines) combines data-fetching, filtering, drag/drop adapters, event listeners, lightbox integration, and rendering in one component; related orchestration hook `useTimelineOrchestrator.ts` (~663 lines) also carries many concerns (zoom, drag, selection, drop, pending frames, endpoint drag, structure-video placement).

Conceptually similar selection UX is duplicated in two separate `ShotSelector` implementations with different interaction contracts and composition (`src/shared/components/ShotSelector.tsx` and `src/shared/components/ImageGenerationForm/components/ShotSelector.tsx`).

Server-side complete-task child-generation logic still clusters extraction, branching orchestration, persistence prep, and heavy debug-state logging in one file (`supabase/functions/complete_task/generation-child.ts`), while param-shape normalization is partly separated but still spread (`supabase/functions/complete_task/params.ts`).

Duplicate test suites exist in parallel directories with near-identical bodies: `src/features/gallery/hooks/__tests__/useGalleryPageState.test.ts` and `src/shared/hooks/__tests__/useGalleryPageState.test.ts`.

Another duplicated pair exists for autosave settings: `src/shared/hooks/__tests__/useAutoSaveSettings.test.ts` and `src/shared/settings/hooks/__tests__/useAutoSaveSettings.test.ts`, but they use different mock import conventions (`@/shared/lib/errorHandler` vs `@/shared/lib/errorHandling/handleError`, `@/shared/lib/deepEqual` vs `@/shared/lib/utils/deepEqual`).

Dark mode tests are split across two locations (`src/shared/hooks/__tests__/useDarkMode.test.ts` and `src/shared/hooks/theme/__tests__/useDarkMode.test.ts`) with different scope and mocking style, indicating unresolved test placement conventions.

Within related task flows, error signaling is mixed: `supabase/functions/complete_task/generation.ts` returns `null` for skip paths (`category === 'orchestration'`, `skip_generation`) while also throwing in catch paths.
`supabase/functions/complete_task/orchestrator.ts` suppresses DB errors by logging and returning `null` in `findSiblingSegments`, and the caller treats `!allSegments` as a normal early-return path.

`src/shared/hooks/useGenerationMutations.ts` mostly throws `Error` with normalized messages, but one path throws raw Supabase error (`throw variantError`), creating inconsistent downstream error shape.
Auth-related hooks use divergent patterns: `src/pages/Home/hooks/auth/useAuthReferralFinalize.ts` routes through `runAuthSupabaseOperation` with structured context/error code handling.

`src/pages/Home/hooks/useDiscordSignIn.ts` directly calls `supabase.auth.signInWithOAuth` and reports errors via generic `handleError` context (`HomePage`) instead of the auth operation wrapper pattern.
The mixed auth operation patterns make auth failure handling/telemetry non-uniform across adjacent auth flows.
authorization_consistency.unreported_risk: Other auth-adjacent hooks outside this batch may also bypass the shared auth-operation wrapper, which can fragment auditability and incident debugging.

findings.summary: Supabase client module executes side effects at import and mutates globals.
evidence: `src/integrations/supabase/client.ts` eagerly exports `supabase = getOrCreateSupabaseClient()` so any importer triggers initialization.
Initialization path installs instrumentation, sets `window.__supabase_client__`/`window.supabase`, and calls `initializeSupabaseRuntime(client)`, creating order-sensitive startup coupling.
findings.suggestion: Export a lazy accessor (for example `getSupabaseClient()`) and move instrumentation/runtime boot into an explicit app bootstrap path; keep global debug registration behind a dedicated dev-only initializer so feature modules do not trigger boot side effects by import alone.

findings.summary: Core error handler is coupled to UI toast presentation.
evidence: `handleError.ts` imports `toast` from UI layer and decides toast rendering in the core handler.
Non-UI hooks like `useMediaGalleryHandlers` call `handleError`, so domain/shared logic inherits UI coupling transitively.
findings.suggestion: Split error normalization/logging from presentation: keep a pure `handleErrorCore` in `shared/lib/errorHandling` and provide a UI adapter (for example `handleErrorWithToast`) in a UI-facing module; update callers to choose the layer explicitly.

findings.summary: Install instructions modal file mixes orchestration with large mockup implementation surface.
evidence: `InstallInstructionsModal.tsx` is 441 LOC and contains many platform/browser-specific visual mockup components plus modal behavior in one file.
Home page composition already uses separate pane components (`CreativePartnerPane`), but install flow keeps a broad mixed-responsibility unit, reducing ownership clarity.
findings.suggestion: Extract mockup primitives and per-platform instruction presenters into dedicated files (for example `components/install-mockups/*` and `components/install-steps/*`), leaving `InstallInstructionsModal` as a thin orchestrator over selected renderer + copy.

findings.summary: Deprecated type shim remains in active dependency path, blurring type ownership.
evidence: `src/types/shots.ts` declares deprecation and re-exports from `@/types/generationAndShots`.
`useMediaGalleryHandlers.ts` still imports `Shot` from `@/types/shots`, extending the transitional surface into active modules.
findings.suggestion: Migrate assigned consumers to import directly from `@/types/generationAndShots`, then enforce with a lint rule or codemod check to prevent new `@/types/shots` imports before removing the shim.

findings.summary: Two separate task-row-to-domain mappers duplicate the same schema translation logic.
evidence: `useTasks.ts` exports `mapDbTaskToTask` mapping `task_type/created_at/...` to camelCase Task fields.
`generationTaskBridge.ts` defines `mapDbTaskRowToTask` with the same field-by-field mapping and null/undefined normalization.
findings.suggestion: Extract a single shared mapper (and shared `TaskDbRow` type) in a task-domain utility, then import it from both hooks/bridge paths.

findings.summary: Timestamp hook family adds multiple thin wrappers with minimal behavioral separation.
evidence: `useCompletedTimestamp` and `useProcessingTimestamp` both call `useLiveRelativeTimestamp` with mostly a formatter and renamed input field.
`useTaskTimestamp` wraps `useUpdatingTimestamp` with a small abbreviation override, adding another API alias layer.
findings.suggestion: Consolidate to one configurable `useRelativeTimestamp` API with named presets/constants to reduce wrapper proliferation.

findings.summary: Image and video lightbox flows duplicate large orchestration seams and prop-bag composition.
evidence: Both image and video paths construct nearly identical `useLightboxWorkflowProps` argument trees (`panel`, `shotWorkflow`, `actions`, `buttonGroupProps`) and then render `LightboxLayout` with the produced layout props.
The shared layout consumes broad grouped props (`props.buttonGroupProps.*`, `props.segmentSlotMode`) while feature-specific wiring remains duplicated at call sites.
findings.suggestion: Introduce a shared lightbox render-model factory for common seam assembly, leaving only media-specific deltas in image/video modules.

findings.summary: Adapter-heavy callback normalization hides upstream contract differences instead of simplifying seams.
evidence: `useShotImagesEditorCallbacks` adds wrappers that convert exceptions to `false` and absent creators to `{ shotId: '', shotName: '' }`, creating implicit fallback semantics.
`useButtonGroupProps` builds grouped objects mostly by forwarding existing values, adding an extra mapping layer without meaningful transformation.
findings.suggestion: Standardize collaborator interfaces (return types and error semantics) and remove adapters where contracts can be made direct.

findings.summary: Gallery page-state test suite is duplicated in two locations, creating parallel maintenance paths.
evidence: Files are identical (same imports, mocks, and test cases; 226 lines each).
Future behavior changes require synchronized edits in two files, raising drift risk.
findings.suggestion: Keep one canonical suite and remove/redirect the duplicate via shared test helper if both paths must remain addressable.

findings.summary: Task builders repeat similar low-level validation/build patterns across modules.
evidence: Each module repeats guard logic for required fields, URL/seed/range checks, and batch fan-out patterns with local variations.
Payload assembly uses repeated manual conditional assignment blocks, increasing inconsistency risk when shared task semantics evolve.
findings.suggestion: Extract reusable validator primitives and shared payload composition helpers in `taskCreation` (e.g., URL/seed/loras validators, batch task fan-out utility).

findings.summary: Two public generation-task modules expose the same API names but incompatible cache payload contracts.
evidence: `generationTaskBridge.preloadGenerationTaskMappings` caches `{ taskId }` only (src/shared/lib/generationTaskBridge.ts:161-163), while `generationTaskCache.preloadGenerationTaskMappings` caches `{ taskId, status }` (src/shared/lib/generationTaskCache.ts:53-56).
Both modules export identically named entry points for preload/enhance (`preloadGenerationTaskMappings`, `enhanceGenerationsWithTaskData`), so call sites can silently bind to different semantics.
Tests for each module assert different expected cache shapes, confirming contract divergence rather than accidental drift.
findings.suggestion: Deprecate `generationTaskBridge` and route all callers through repository-backed `generationTaskCache`; enforce a single `GenerationTaskCacheEntry` contract and add a compatibility adapter only at migration boundaries.

findings.summary: Video request assembly uses an overloaded boundary API that accepts two competing call shapes in one function.
evidence: `buildTravelRequestBody` accepts grouped params (`motionParams`, `generationParams`, `seedParams`) and legacy flat fields simultaneously, then resolves fallback precedence internally (src/tools/.../generateVideoService.ts:463-542).

The function comment explicitly marks the flat fields as backward-compat for tests/older call sites, indicating intentional multi-shape support at a core boundary (src/tools/.../generateVideoService.ts:473-489).
A second payload builder (`buildTaskParams`) in `segmentSettingsUtils` maps similar domain fields with separate logic/defaults, increasing API inconsistency across adjacent seams.
findings.suggestion: Split into explicit `buildTravelRequestBodyV2` (grouped params only) and a thin legacy adapter; centralize shared field mapping between segment and travel builders into one canonical payload translator.

findings.summary: Generation invalidation seam exposes inconsistent lifecycle behavior across hook, sync helper, and variant invalidator APIs.
evidence: `InvalidationOptions.reason` is required but never used in invalidation execution (src/shared/hooks/invalidation/useGenerationInvalidation.ts:7-56).

`useInvalidateGenerations` debounces per-shot with cancellable timeout refs, but `invalidateGenerationsSync` uses uncancelled fire-and-forget `setTimeout`, yielding different timing/cleanup semantics for equivalent inputs (src/shared/hooks/invalidation/useGenerationInvalidation.ts:60-97).

`invalidateVariantChange` adds an awaited delay plus targeted refetch/invalidate flow, creating a third operational mode in the same API family (src/shared/hooks/invalidation/useGenerationInvalidation.ts:105-141).
findings.suggestion: Unify invalidation execution behind one scheduler primitive (with cancellation tokens and reason logging), and make the sync helper a strict wrapper over that scheduler instead of separate timer logic.

findings.summary: `getStoragePublicUrl` reports existence without verifying object presence, conflicting with consumer expectations.
evidence: Function comment states existence is not verified, but return shape still includes `exists: true|false`, implying a truth check (supabase/functions/complete_task/storage.ts:177-190).
Implementation sets `exists: true` whenever `getPublicUrl` returns a URL, which can be constructed for nonexistent paths.
Current tests only verify exports and do not assert this contract behavior, leaving high-risk ambiguity unguarded (supabase/functions/complete_task/storage.test.ts:1-10).
findings.suggestion: Rename return field to `urlGenerated` (or rename API to `buildStoragePublicUrl`) or perform an explicit existence check; add branch tests for nonexistent objects and error paths.

findings.summary: Shared edge helpers expose multiple incompatible error/response shapes, increasing caller branching at request boundaries.
evidence: `ensureRequestMethod` returns plain text `Response` on 405, while `edgeErrorResponse` returns JSON envelope with error metadata (supabase/functions/_shared/edgeRequest.ts:49-102).

`parseJsonBody` returns typed `OperationResult`, but `parseJsonBodyStrict` converts to `{ body?, errorResponse? }`, forcing callers to handle two contracts in one utility area (supabase/functions/_shared/edgeRequest.ts:125-196).
`rateLimitResponse` returns a different JSON schema (`error`, `retryAfter`, `message`) and header conventions, adding another boundary variant (supabase/functions/_shared/rateLimit.ts:146-163).
findings.suggestion: Adopt one edge response envelope and one parse contract (prefer `OperationResult` end-to-end), then provide thin conversion helpers only for legacy handlers.

findings.summary: Deprecated `@/types/shots` compatibility alias is still in active use, delaying migration closure.
evidence: src/types/shots.ts is marked `@deprecated` with explicit remove-by `2026-05-31`.
src/shared/components/ShotImageManager/utils/image-utils.ts imports `GenerationRow` from `@/types/shots`, so active call sites still rely on the deprecated bridge.
findings.suggestion: Replace remaining `@/types/shots` imports with `@/types/generationAndShots`, then remove the compatibility re-export once import count reaches zero.

findings.summary: Legacy travel-between-images payload fields remain first-class in type contracts, preserving dual schema paths.
evidence: legacyStructureVideo.ts exposes deprecated fields (`structure_video_*`, `uni3c_*`, `use_uni3c`) plus a shim removal contract.
ImageGenerationForm/types.ts retains deprecated settings fields for older storage payloads, extending coexistence of old and new representations.
findings.suggestion: Add a single migration boundary (read-old/write-new), stop emitting deprecated fields in runtime writes, and schedule deletion of legacy interfaces immediately after telemetry confirms no old payload reads.

findings.summary: Two separate helpers implement overlapping aspect-ratio derivation and fallback behavior.
evidence: Both helpers parse project ratio via `parseRatio(...)` and fallback to square `'1'`.
`imageAspectRatio.ts` additionally parses metadata/originalParams resolution, while ShotImageManager keeps a slimmer variant; this split invites divergence.
findings.suggestion: Consolidate ratio derivation into one shared utility with optional metadata parsing flags, and make ShotImageManager consume it directly.

findings.summary: Comment-heavy, thin-wrapper helpers add ceremony without proportional behavioral value.
evidence: `cn.ts` has contract-level doc framing around a direct `twMerge(clsx(...))` pass-through.
`useUpdatingTimestamp.ts` includes extensive explanatory comments and example blocks around mostly delegated formatting behavior.
`caseConversion.ts` includes long explanatory sections for a small key-transform helper.
findings.suggestion: Trim comments to non-obvious constraints only, and keep wrappers only when they enforce policy or hide unstable dependencies; otherwise prefer direct usage.

findings.summary: State toggle uses captured value instead of functional update, reducing robustness under batched updates.
evidence: `useTextCase.ts` defines `const toggle = () => setPreserveUserText(!preserveUserText);` using closure state.
Adjacent shared hooks in this batch are utility primitives expected to be resilient; this pattern is a weaker low-level update style.
findings.suggestion: Use functional form `setPreserveUserText(prev => !prev)` for deterministic toggling under concurrent/batched React updates.

findings.summary: Low-fan-in root config files are stragglers despite existing config packages.
evidence: Each assigned root config file is peripheral with fan_in < 5 per holistic_context.structure.root_files.
Repository already contains concern-aligned config directories (`config/testing/`, `config/vite/`, and `config/tailwind/...`), so root placement duplicates package boundaries.
vitest.config.ts depends on another root config (`./vitest.edge.config`) instead of a single organized testing config namespace.
findings.suggestion: Staged reorg plan: (1) Create/confirm `config/testing`, `config/vite`, and `config/tailwind` entrypoints; (2) move `vitest.config.ts`, `vitest.edge.config.ts`, `vitest.edge.smoke.config.ts` to `config/testing/`; move `vite.config.ts` to `config/vite/`; move `tailwind.config.ts` to `config/tailwind/`; (3) add minimal root shim files only if required by tool defaults, otherwise update scripts to point to new paths; (4) run import/script updates and validation commands: `rg -n "vitest\.edge\.config|vitest\.config|vite\.config|tailwind\.config" .`, `npm run test -- --config config/testing/vitest.config.ts`, `npm run test:edge -- --config config/testing/vitest.edge.config.ts`, `npm run build -- --config config/vite/vite.config.ts` (or equivalent project scripts).

findings.summary: Feature orchestration hooks are placed in `shared/hooks`, obscuring true ownership.
evidence: `useAIInteractionService` is AI-domain workflow logic (prompt generation/edit/summarization via edge invocation), not a generic shared primitive.
`useAdjacentPagePreloader` explicitly targets gallery pagination orchestration and mentions `MediaGallery` behavior in comments.
`useApiKeys` encapsulates settings/account credential persistence workflow and query-key policy, indicating feature ownership.
Co-located tests under `src/shared/hooks/__tests__` reinforce shared placement of domain-specific behavior.
findings.suggestion: Staged reorg plan: (1) create feature-owned hook folders (for example `src/features/ai/hooks`, `src/features/gallery/hooks`, `src/features/settings/hooks`); (2) move hooks in order of least-coupled first: `useAIInteractionService` -> AI, `useApiKeys` -> settings, `useAdjacentPagePreloader` -> gallery; (3) move matching tests with each hook into feature-local `__tests__`; (4) add temporary re-export shims in old `src/shared/hooks/*` paths for one transition PR, then remove after callers migrate; (5) run update/validation commands: `rg -n "@/shared/hooks/use(AIInteractionService|AdjacentPagePreloader|ApiKeys)" src`, `rg -n "from '../use(AIInteractionService|AdjacentPagePreloader|ApiKeys)'" src`, `npm run test -- src/features/ai src/features/gallery src/features/settings`, then full `npm run test`.

findings.summary: Supabase client module performs hidden runtime initialization at import time.
evidence: `supabase` is created eagerly at module load (`export const supabase = getOrCreateSupabaseClient();`).
Client creation path mutates global window symbols and initializes runtime/instrumentation, making import order part of runtime behavior.
Top-level docs do not state this contract, so consumer modules cannot reason about side effects from import alone.
findings.suggestion: Split `client.ts` into explicit `getSupabaseClient()` (lazy) and `initializeSupabase()` (bootstrap-only side effects), keep `supabase` export as deprecated compatibility wrapper for one release, and document bootstrap ownership in README architecture docs so side effects are no longer implicit.

findings.summary: Deprecated `shots` shim has a sunset contract but no enforceable migration gate.
evidence: `src/types/shots.ts` declares deprecation and remove-by date but still acts as a broad re-export shim.
Holistic context identifies this file as heavily imported (~200 importers), signaling policy drift from intended temporary compatibility role.
README documents quality references but omits a concrete compatibility/removal enforcement workflow.
findings.suggestion: Add a repository-level compatibility policy section in README (deprecation owner, SLA, and removal gate), then enforce with `no-restricted-imports` banning `@/types/shots` for new code and a CI check (`rg "@/types/shots" src`) that must trend to zero before removing the shim.

findings.summary: Generic `shared/lib/utils` barrel weakens package ownership and import boundaries.
evidence: `utils.ts` is a broad alias entrypoint rather than a concern-scoped module.
`button.tsx` imports `cn` through that barrel, coupling UI primitives to a general shared-lib namespace.
Structure signals show heavy `src/shared/components/ui/ → src/shared/lib/` coupling (36 edges), consistent with boundary leakage.
findings.suggestion: Staged reorg plan: Stage 1 target folders: create `src/shared/components/ui/lib/` for UI-only class helpers and keep string helpers in `src/shared/lib/stringFormatting.ts`; Stage 2 move order: (1) move `cn` access to UI-local entrypoint, (2) update `button.tsx` and other `src/shared/components/ui/*` imports, (3) shrink `src/shared/lib/utils.ts` to temporary compatibility re-export, (4) remove barrel after call sites are migrated; Stage 3 import-update/validation commands: `rg "@/shared/lib/utils" src/shared/components/ui src/shared/components`, apply import rewrites, then run `npm run lint` and `npm run test -- src/shared/components/ui/button.tsx`.

findings.summary: Image lightbox edit flow relies on oversized orchestration contracts that mix mode composition, state shaping, and UI-facing API assembly.
evidence: `useImageEditOrchestrator` accepts a very broad prop object (lines 32-92) spanning media identity, variant lifecycle, persistence, and LoRA config, then builds a large context payload (lines 392-524).

`ImageLightbox.tsx` constructs multiple intermediate memo objects (`lightboxCore`, `lightboxMedia`, `lightboxVariants`, `lightboxNavigation`) and then re-bundles them into `useLightboxWorkflowProps` with many pass-through handlers (lines 544-736).
Placeholder values (`isFlippedHorizontally=false`, `isSaving=false`) are injected into the central edit context (lines 388-391), signaling an interface larger than currently owned behavior.
findings.suggestion: Define explicit per-mode adapters (inpaint/magic/reposition/img2img) with narrow typed contracts, then compose them behind a smaller `ImageEditFacade` consumed by `ImageLightbox`. Move UI-workflow prop assembly out of `ImageLightbox` into a dedicated mapper so container responsibilities are reduced to composition.

findings.summary: Timeline layers interaction orchestration, query integration, and rendering in one component/hook pair, creating a high-change blast radius.
evidence: `Timeline.tsx` defines a very large prop surface (lines 37-102) and performs filtering, sorting, drop adapters, task lookup, image preloading effects, event subscriptions, reset logic, and lightbox wiring before render (roughly lines 167-581).

`useTimelineOrchestrator.ts` bundles multiple concerns in one hook: zoom management, drag lifecycle, drop interceptors, duplicate collision logic, tap-to-move, structure video placement, endpoint drag, and computed pair data (lines 183-662).

The orchestrator includes sync-by-timer mechanics (`setTimeout(...forceUpdate...)` lines 340-343 and delayed scroll adjustment lines 373-385), indicating lifecycle complexity concentrated in one abstraction.
findings.suggestion: Split timeline into explicit layers: `TimelineDataController` (queries/derivations), `TimelineInteractionController` (drag/zoom/drop), and `TimelineView`. Keep each hook single-purpose and return focused contracts; move drop/duplicate frame-calculation policies into dedicated pure services with tests.

findings.summary: Two separate ShotSelector implementations encode overlapping domain behavior with divergent interaction models and styling contracts.
evidence: `src/shared/components/ShotSelector.tsx` uses popover+command search, optional quick-create success flow, controlled/uncontrolled open behavior, and navigation callbacks.
`src/shared/components/ImageGenerationForm/components/ShotSelector.tsx` reimplements selection with `Select`, local sorting, inline jump link, and separate clear/create affordances.
Both components represent the same core domain selection concern (`shot` selection + optional create/jump actions) but expose different APIs and UX semantics, increasing integration inconsistency.
findings.suggestion: Create a single shared `ShotSelector` domain component with composable render variants (`compact`, `form`, `lightbox`) and a common behavior contract for create/clear/jump flows. Keep styling as wrappers while centralizing option sorting/filtering and event semantics.

findings.summary: Child-generation completion logic still mixes orchestration, mutation, extraction, and debugging responsibilities in one handler module.
evidence: `handleChildGeneration` mutates incoming task params (`taskData.params._isSingleSegmentCase` and reassignment via `extractSegmentSpecificParams`) while also coordinating variant creation and fallback generation insertion (lines 60-131).
`generation-child.ts` contains persistence logic plus large debug logging helper `logSegmentMasterState` (lines 390-481) in the same module as core write-path behavior.

`params.ts` centralizes some extraction, but path-resolution knowledge still appears in child-generation flow (e.g., direct fallback chains for `pair_shot_generation_id` in lines 83-90), showing normalization policy split across files.
findings.suggestion: Introduce an immutable `TaskParamNormalizer` stage that produces a typed `NormalizedSegmentTask` consumed by a smaller `ChildGenerationService`. Move debug-state formatting/logging to a separate diagnostics module and keep write-path modules focused on deterministic orchestration + persistence.

findings.summary: Parallel duplicated test trees have drifted in imports and mocking conventions
evidence: The two `useGalleryPageState` test files are effectively identical line-for-line but live under different feature roots.

The two `useAutoSaveSettings` tests are near-duplicates yet use different dependency paths (`@/shared/lib/errorHandler` vs `@/shared/lib/errorHandling/handleError`, `@/shared/lib/deepEqual` vs `@/shared/lib/utils/deepEqual`).
Dark mode tests are duplicated across `hooks` and `hooks/theme`, with different test scope and mocks.
findings.suggestion: Pick one canonical test location convention (feature-local or shared), delete duplicate copies, and normalize all test mocks to current module paths in a single migration commit.

findings.summary: Complete-task modules mix thrown failures with null-return suppression
evidence: `findSiblingSegments` logs query errors and returns `null` (`orchestrator.ts`), and upstream logic treats missing segments as a normal return path instead of a failure.
`createGenerationFromTask` uses nullable control returns for skip cases but throws on operational errors (`generation.ts`), creating inconsistent caller expectations for failure vs non-action.
findings.suggestion: Define a single contract for these orchestration helpers (e.g., explicit discriminated result `{status:'skipped'|'ok'|'error'}` or throw-on-error everywhere) and apply it consistently across `orchestrator.ts` and `generation.ts`.

findings.summary: Sibling auth flows use different authorization-operation handling protocols
evidence: `useAuthReferralFinalize` wraps Supabase RPC in `runAuthSupabaseOperation` with explicit auth context and error code.
`useDiscordSignIn` bypasses that wrapper and emits generic `handleError` calls with `context: 'HomePage'`, reducing auth-specific consistency.
findings.suggestion: Adopt one auth operation wrapper for both flows (including OAuth start), with standardized auth context/error codes and consistent surface for caller handling.

findings.summary: Consumers import `getSupabaseClient`, but the declared client module does not export it.
evidence: `src/integrations/supabase/client.ts` exports `supabase` only.
Assigned consumers import `getSupabaseClient` from that same module path, creating a broken/ambiguous API contract.
findings.suggestion: Make the client API explicit and singular: export `getSupabaseClient` from `client.ts` (or migrate all consumers to `supabase`/runtime accessors), then run `rg -n "getSupabaseClient|from '@/integrations/supabase/client'" src` and `pnpm -s tsc --noEmit` to validate callsites.

findings.summary: Legacy and new Supabase runtime paths coexist without a completed cutover.
evidence: Instrumentation entrypoints still delegate to `*Legacy` functions with comments indicating manager removal.
A separate runtime module exists with explicit initializer/getter while active app usage centers on eager singleton client path.
findings.suggestion: Define a single runtime bootstrap contract and retire compatibility layers in phases: (1) choose one entrypoint (`initializeSupabaseClientRuntime` or eager singleton), (2) route all instrumentation/auth init through that entrypoint, (3) delete `*Legacy` wrappers after import migration, (4) verify with `rg -n "Legacy|initializeSupabaseClientRuntime|getOrCreateSupabaseClient" src` and full typecheck/tests.

findings.summary: Supabase integration crosses boundaries via mutable globals and global monkeypatching.
evidence: Auth/client state is shared via `window.__supabase_client__`, `window.supabase`, and `window.__AUTH_MANAGER__`.
Instrumentation rewrites browser/global behavior (`window.onerror`, `window.WebSocket`, `console.warn`) from module init paths.
findings.suggestion: Move to dependency-injected runtime services: expose a single `SupabaseRuntime` object, pass it to auth/hooks via providers, and confine monkeypatch logic behind explicit dev-only setup hooks called once at bootstrap.

findings.summary: Hook error contracts mix thrown exceptions, swallowed failures, and sentinel returns.
evidence: `useAIInteractionService` catches and returns `null`/`[]` on error.
`useCredits` throws new `Error(...)` for query failures and relies on react-query error channel.
`App` silently ignores a startup exception from `getNetworkStatusManager`.
findings.suggestion: Standardize per-layer contracts: query/mutation functions should throw typed errors, UI adapters may map to user-friendly fallback states, and startup catches should at least emit `reportRuntimeError` with context instead of empty catches.

findings.summary: Several wrappers add naming churn but no behavior or policy.
evidence: `installRealtimeInstrumentation` and `installWindowOnlyInstrumentation` are direct delegations to legacy methods.
Feature hooks repeatedly define `getSupabase` pass-through helpers that only call imported `getSupabaseClient`.
findings.suggestion: Collapse no-op wrapper layers: inline or remove `*Legacy` delegation where no alternate implementation exists, and replace local one-line `getSupabase` helpers with direct shared accessor usage unless hook-specific behavior is added.

findings.summary: `src/pages/Home/components` is an overloaded flat directory with mixed responsibilities.
evidence: Holistic structure reports `src/pages/Home/components/` with 26 files, exceeding the >10 mixed-concern threshold.
Single folder contains hero shell, pane system, install browser mockups, and motion-comparison subsystem files, reducing navigability and ownership clarity.
findings.suggestion: Staged reorg plan: Target folders: `src/pages/Home/components/hero/`, `src/pages/Home/components/panes/`, `src/pages/Home/components/install/`, `src/pages/Home/components/motion/`. Move order: (1) move motion files (`MotionComparison*` and related controller/overlays), (2) move install modal + browser mockup helpers, (3) move pane files (`CreativePartnerPane`, `ExamplesPane`, `PhilosophyPane`, shared pane primitives), (4) move hero files (`HeroSection`, `HeroCtaContent`, `GoldSpotlight`, `HomeBackground`). Import-update/validation commands: `rg -n "pages/Home/components/" src`, then apply path updates, then `pnpm -s tsc --noEmit` and `pnpm -s vitest --run`.
